title: 10 best herbal creams for hemorrhoids

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with hemorrhoids because of their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that work together to reduce swelling, pain, and itching associated with hemorrhoids. It also provides a protective barrier on the affected area, allowing for easy passage of stool while reducing irritation. This natural remedy helps to calm and comfort the skin, providing fast and effective relief from hemorrhoid discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids by suppressing prostaglandins and leukotrienes.
Soothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's gel content provides a protective barrier that soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing of hemorrhoid tissues.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Aloe vera's antimicrobial compounds, such as salicylic acid and lupeol, help prevent infection and reduce the risk of complications by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which help soothe and reduce pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Rhyzomes: Containing mucilages that provide a protective barrier, helping to cushion and calm irritated skin around the anal area.
remedy_recipe: Gather necessary ingredients including pure aloe vera gel, coconut oil, olive oil, beeswax, calendula petals and vitamin e oil.
Combine one tablespoon of aloe vera gel, two tablespoons of coconut oil, and two tablespoons of olive oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Melt two teaspoons of beeswax in a double boiler or in the microwave for 10-second intervals until fully dissolved.
Slowly pour the melted beeswax mixture into the aloe vera gel mixture while whisking continuously until well combined and smooth.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for at least one hour before transferring it to an airtight container for storage and use within six weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with hemorrhoids because of their unique combination of natural ingredients that provide soothing relief from itching, burning, and discomfort. The astringent properties of witch hazel help reduce inflammation and shrink swollen veins, while the herbal extracts work to calm irritated skin and promote healing. Additionally, the gentle, non-irritating texture of these creams allows for easy application and absorption, providing fast and effective relief from hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_properties: Astringent Properties: The tannins present in witch hazel help to constrict and shrink swollen tissues, providing relief from itching, burning, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Witch hazel's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.

Antiseptic Properties: The antibacterial and antifungal properties of witch hazel help to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for wound healing, reducing the risk of complications and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are rich in tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and shrink swollen hemorrhoid tissues.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain witch hazel's active compound, gallotannin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in soothing and healing hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves and twigs from a sustainable source.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 150°f for 6 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried witch hazel into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered witch hazel with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Stir the mixture thoroughly and allow it to infuse for 2 weeks before filtering and transferring to glass jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with hemorrhoids because of their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The saponins present in marshmallows create a protective barrier on the affected area, reducing irritation and discomfort. This cream also helps to retain moisture, keeping the anal area hydrated and softening stool, making bowel movements easier and less painful. Additionally, the natural ingredients in herbal marshmallow creams can help to shrink swollen veins and reduce hemorrhoid swelling, providing fast and effective relief from symptoms.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow cream contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and shields irritated tissue, providing relief from hemorrhoidal discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal marshmallow creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin or flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.

Softering and protective effects: The mucilage in marshmallow cream helps to soften and protect the skin around the anus, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by hemorrhoidal itching, burning, and bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and protect the inflamed tissue around hemorrhoids.
Rhyzomes: High in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root althaea officinalis and dry it properly.
Grind dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix one part powdered marshmallow root with two parts petroleum jelly in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low temperature for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into a clean container and allow it to cool completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with hemorrhoids because its active compound, calendula, possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The cream's natural astringent effects help to shrink swollen veins and reduce pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties promote healing and regeneration of damaged skin, making it an effective topical treatment for reducing inflammation, itching, and bleeding caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold creams contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which reduce inflammation and swelling associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams help protect the skin around the anus from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications such as infection or scarring.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's saponins have been shown to enhance wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and reducing scar formation, which can help repair damaged tissue around hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain a high amount of omega-3 fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of damaged tissues in the rectal area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven.
Combine 20g of dried calendula petals with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily, to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then add beeswax and emulsifier.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180°f for 15 minutes until the wax is fully melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as isobutylamides and triterpenoid saponins, reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in purple coneflower creams, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the anal area, promoting healing and reducing pain.

Wound-healing: Purple coneflower's saponins and polyphenols have been shown to enhance wound closure by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and reducing scarring, which can aid in the recovery process from hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Barks: Contain tannins and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the echinacea plant material using a food dehydrator or sun drying method for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried echinacea plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Melt 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Combine the echinacea powder with the melted wax-oil mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with hemorrhoids because they contain Echinacea, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms irritated tissues. The cream's active ingredients also promote blood vessel contraction, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, the herb's antioxidant properties help to heal damaged tissue and promote collagen production, strengthening the affected area and reducing the risk of further irritation. This natural remedy provides effective relief from hemorrhoid symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and improved overall well-being.

Maidenhair tree creams helps with hemorrhoids because it contains compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as madecassol and saponins, work to reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area, providing immediate relief from itching, burning, and pain. Additionally, the cream's emollient texture helps to moisturize and soften the skin, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further irritation. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for managing hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoid compounds in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's terpenes and flavonoids can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the affected area and reducing symptoms such as itching, burning, and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in Ginkgo biloba creams can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms and contribute to chronic inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, making them an ideal component for treating hemorrhoid symptoms.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Dry and grind 250 grams of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 100 grams of beeswax, 50 grams of coconut oil, and 20 grams of shea butter in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Melt the mixture by heating it over low heat for 30 minutes to combine the ingredients thoroughly and dissolve the wax.
Allow the mixture to cool until it reaches a temperature of around 60°c and then stir in 5 drops of tea tree oil and 2 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars, store them in a cool dark place, and allow the cream to set for at least 24 hours before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with hemorrhoids because they contain berberine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms irritated tissues. The cream's antiseptic properties also help to reduce swelling and promote healing, making it an effective treatment for external hemorrhoids. Additionally, goldenseal's antimicrobial activity may help to prevent infection and combat the bacteria that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce swelling and prevent infection in hemorrhoid-prone areas.

Berberine's antioxidant properties: Berberine's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to tissue damage and inflammation in the rectal area, reducing the risk of hemorrhoids.

Goldenseal's astringent properties: The astringent compounds in goldenseal, such as berberine and hydrastinine, help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which can alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids like itching, burning, and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Goldenseal contain berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and heal inflamed hemorrhoid tissue.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Goldenseal have been traditionally used to reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids due to their ability to stimulate the body's natural healing processes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hydrastis canadensis roots in late summer or early fall when they are fully mature.
Clean and dry the harvested roots then cut them into small pieces.
Combine 20 grams of dried hydrastis canadensis root with 100 ml of organic jojoba oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily to release the herbal properties.
Strain and filter the infused oil then add 10% beeswax by weight and blend until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with hemorrhoids because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The creams also contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes the mucous membranes and relaxes the muscles in the anal area, reducing strain and pressure on the hemorrhoid tissues. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice cream helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from pain, itching, and discomfort.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and calm irritated skin, making it effective in reducing hemorrhoid symptoms.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal quality.
Grind 100 grams of harvested root material into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 200 milliliters of distilled water and 20 grams of coconut oil, heating the mixture to 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding solids after 2 hours.
Allow the resulting cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 24 hours before transferring it to an air-tight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with hemorrhoids because its unique properties provide relief from discomfort, itching, and inflammation. Barks: Contain astringent compounds that help reduce inflammation and shrink swollen hemorrhoid tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves measuring one ounce per patient treatment.
Dry the leaves in shade at room temperature for seven days ensuring crispness.
Grind one tablespoon of dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix two tablespoons of coconut oil with four drops of lavender essential oil adding powdered ulmus rubra.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature within sixty minutes.

remedy_recipe: Collect dried cinchona officinalis leaves of good quality weighing about 20 grams.
Combine collected leaves with 100 milliliters of boiling water allowing it to steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or coffee filter discarding the solids and retaining the infusion.
Mix 50 milliliters of the strained infusion with 25 grams of petroleum jelly and 10 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax melts taking approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with hemorrhoids because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated tissues. The cream's astringent agents constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and bleeding. Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Plantain's antimicrobial properties help prevent infection and promote healing by destroying bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms.

Soothing and protective mucilages: The gel-like substance in plantain cream coats and protects the affected area, reducing irritation, discomfort, and pain, while also promoting a healthy environment for healing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried plantago major leaves from reputable sources to ensure quality and potency.
Mix 50g of dried plantago major leaves with 500ml of warm water in a heatproof bowl.

Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding solids and transfer to an ice bath.
Once cooled, add 20g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil to the liquid and stir until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its unique combination of medicinal compounds. The cream's active ingredients, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, work together to soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale, drying them in shade at room temperature for 2 days.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking approximately 20 minutes.
Combine one part powdered symphytum officinale with two parts petroleum jelly in a double boiler, stirring constantly for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature that is safe to apply topically, about 1 hour.
Transfer the cooled mixture into sterile jars, labeling them with date and contents, and storing them in refrigerator at 40°f or below for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with hemorrhoids because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plant's flavonoids and taraxasterol work together to reduce swelling, ease discomfort, and promote healing in the affected area. The cream's moisturizing qualities also help to soften stools, making them easier to pass, which can alleviate constipation-related hemorrhoid issues. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 2 hours or air drying for several days.
Combine 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves with 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and infuse in a glass jar at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl discarding solids and mixing with 10ml of beeswax and 5ml of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into sterilized cream jars and allow to cool at room temperature before capping and labeling for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with hemorrhoids because they offer a soothing and calming effect on irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinones, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Rhyzomes: Contains bioactive compounds that aid in soothing itching, burning, and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves measure out 2 tablespoons.
Combine the harvested botanicals with 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil infuse for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discard solids retain liquid.
Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil melt beeswax in double boiler.
Pour the warm mixture into glass jars let cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb guggul, also known as Commiphora mukul, contains compounds that reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids. remedy_recipe: Grind 100g of dried commiphora mukul resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground commiphora mukul with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass bowl.
Add 5% by weight of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated to create a smooth consistency.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to facilitate melting of the beeswax.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then whip the cream using an electric mixer until light and airy, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with hemorrhoids because it provides natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm the affected area. Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal tea tree creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the rectal area, providing relief from painful and uncomfortable hemorrhoids.

remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass containers and utensils by autoclaving for 15 minutes at 121°c.
Combine 200g of petroleum jelly with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 20ml of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Continue heating the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 70°c, then remove from heat and let cool to 40°c.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized containers and label with date and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with hemorrhoids because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The curcumin in turmeric reduces swelling, pain, and itching associated with hemorrhoids, providing instant relief. remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried turmeric powder into a fine consistency using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil with 2 teaspoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Combine the ground turmeric powder with 5 milliliters of coconut oil and stir until well blended.
Gradually add the turmeric-coconut oil mixture to the oil-beeswax mixture while stirring constantly.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and chop them finely to ensure optimal potency and efficiency.
Combine the chopped leaves with 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, pressing on the solids to extract all oils and essences.
Allow the resulting cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with hemorrhoids because they contain the soothing properties of yarrow flowers, which have been used for centuries to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort. The cream's active ingredients, such as chamazulene and apigenin, work together to constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and alleviating pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, yarrow's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help prevent infection and promote healing, providing long-lasting relief from hemorrhoidal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal yarrow creams containing flavonoids and sesquiterpenes help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in herbal yarrow creams help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms, promoting healing and protecting against tissue damage.

Vasoconstrictive property: The compounds found in herbal yarrow creams, such as thymol and terpinen-4-ol, help constrict blood vessels, which can reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of external or internal hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing irritated tissues and reducing swelling around the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and stems in the early morning or evening to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it thinly on paper towels or cloth for 7-10 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried achillea millefolium with 100 milliliters of organic coconut oil in a heatproof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler setup at 60°c to 70°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hemorrhoids are topical ointments made from natural ingredients like aloe vera, witch hazel, and chamomile that provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids. These creams help by reducing inflammation, soothing itching and burning sensations, and promoting healing. Examples of effective herbal creams include Preparation H with aloe vera and tea tree oil, and Hemorrhoid Relief Cream with witch hazel and chamomile. By using these creams, individuals can experience reduced discomfort during bowel movements, improved sleep quality, and increased confidence in their daily activities.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hemorrhoids with herbal creams is to choose products containing ingredients such as witch hazel, aloe vera, and horse chestnut, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and shrink swollen veins. Apply the cream directly to the affected area several times a day, following the product's instructions for dosage and frequency of application.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hemorrhoids that are treated with herbal creams are anal fissures, itching and inflammation in the genital area, and perianal pruritus. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile can provide relief from symptoms such as pain, discomfort, and itchiness. These natural remedies can help soothe and calm the affected areas, reducing inflammation and promoting healing without harsh chemicals or side effects.
